steps.title: Find the perimeter of square X
steps.content:
Square X has side length 12 cm. The perimeter of any square is $4 \times \text{side}$.
Perimeter of X = $4 \times 12 = 48$ cm

steps.title: Find the perimeter of square Y
steps.content:
We're told the perimeter of square Y is 2 times the perimeter of square X.
Perimeter of Y = $2 \times 48 = 96$ cm

steps.title: Find the side length of square Y
steps.content:
Since square Y has perimeter 96 cm, and the perimeter of a square equals $4 \times \text{side}$:
$$96 = 4 \times \text{side}$$
$$\text{side} = \frac{96}{4} = 24 \text{ cm}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
